reread: ts-basic-service SecurityConfig.java corsConfigurer bean (lines 51-56) registers /** with allowedOrigins/Methods/Headers=ALL, allowCredentials(false), maxAge(3600); both candidates describe this same bean under different phrasing.

reread: ts-cancel-service CancelController.java has two separate method-level @CrossOrigin(origins = "*") annotations: one on calculate() at line 32 (cancel/refound/{orderId}) and one on cancelTicket() at line 39 (cancel/{orderId}/{loginId}); the reference records both endpoints as one combined authority atom while the four candidates (two per pass) record them as separate per-endpoint atoms describing the same two annotations.

reread: ts-cancel-service SecurityConfig.java lines 72-73, same swagger antMatcher block as basic-service.

reference records that getToken's success branch returns TokenDto(user.getUserId(), username, token) (TokenServiceImpl.java line 96); neither pass wrote a contract atom for TokenServiceImpl itself (only the TokenService interface's shapedAs atom was captured, matched separately), so no candidate remains to cite for this Impl-level ensures-clause.

reread: TokenServiceImpl.java lines 62-79: when verifyCode is non-empty, a GET to ts-verification-code-service must return true before the flow proceeds to authenticationManager.authenticate(); when empty, the verify call is skipped. Candidate records the same gating condition under a semantic/meansInUse key rather than the reference's contract/requires key.

reference records a compound ensures-clause for cancelOrder's success branch (order status set to CANCEL, refund amount computed, and drawn back to the account); the sub-facts are individually covered elsewhere (state transition, drawback effect, response-status semantics) but neither pass wrote a single contract atom synthesizing this combined outcome for cancelOrder, so no distinct candidate remains to cite here.

reread: CancelServiceImpl.java private calculateRefund(Order) lines 200-235: returns "0.00" on NOTPAID, "0" when now is after the computed travel start time, else price*0.8 formatted with DecimalFormat("0.00").

reread: CancelServiceImpl.java getAuthorizationHeadersFrom() lines 254-260, used at the order/order-other/insidepayment/user call sites; copies the incoming Authorization header when present, omits it otherwise.

reread: CancelServiceImpl.java lines 52-53 and 109-110: order.getStatus() must equal NOTPAID, PAID, or CHANGE for the cancel branch to proceed, otherwise the cancel-not-permitted response is returned. Both candidates record this same branching condition under a cech/sectionValue key rather than the reference's contract/requires key.

reread: ts-common SwaggerConfig.java line 21, @Value("${swagger.controllerPackage}") plus per-service application.yml values (ts-basic-service: fdse.microservice.controller; ts-cancel-service: cancel.controller) confirm the property is supplied per service; candidates describe the injection mechanism without naming the concrete per-service values.

reread: CancelServiceImpl.java cancelFromOtherOrder() lines 263-276.

reread: TokenServiceImpl.java lines 64-71, GET ts-verification-code-service /api/v1/verifycode/verify/{verifyCode}; both candidates (effect axis) describe the same call the reference records under relation axis.

reference records the field-level dependency on auth.security.jwt.JWTProvider; both passes only captured the JWTProvider relationship as a calls-relation on createToken() (already matched to atom:relation:auth-tokenserviceimpl:calls-jwtprovider-createtoken), leaving no separate dependsOn-axis candidate to cite.

reread: UserServiceImpl.java line 33, @Autowired protected PasswordEncoder passwordEncoder, invoked at line 57 passwordEncoder.encode(dto.getPassword()); candidate records the encode() call site, reference records the field-level dependency, same underlying relationship.

reread: TokenServiceImpl.java lines 75-96: status 0 returned on verification-code-rejected (line 77) and credential-rejected (line 87) branches, status 1 with TokenDto returned on the success branch (line 96).

reference records that station existence is checked via an endpoint path segment named id while the value passed is a station-name string (BasicServiceImpl.java checkStationExists/queryForStationId call .../stations/id/{stationName} with a name argument); neither pass wrote an identifiedBy-predicate atom capturing this naming observation.

reread: BasicServiceImpl.java queryForTravel() lines 44-120 and queryForTravels() lines 123-321 set status 1 on the result-carrying branch and status 0 on station/traintype/route/order-mismatch rejection branches; queryForStationId() lines 323-339 returns status 0 with the forwarded upstream message on the not-found branch. reread: CancelController.java cancelTicket() lines 41-51: the caught-exception branch at line 50 returns new Response<>(1, "error", null) - the same status value 1 used on the normal success branch returned directly from the service layer.

reference records that order lookup tries ts-order-service first, then ts-order-other-service on the not-found branch (CancelServiceImpl.java lines 48/104 in cancelOrder, 159/177 in calculateRefund); neither pass wrote an identifiedBy-predicate atom for this fallback lookup pattern, though the underlying getOrderByIdFromOrder/getOrderByIdFromOrderOther calls are separately captured as relation/effect atoms elsewhere.

reread: CancelServiceImpl.java getAccount(String orderId, ...) at line 294, called at line 70 with order.getAccountId().toString() - the parameter named orderId carries an account id value.

reread: CancelServiceImpl.java cancelOrder() lines 46-141 and calculateRefund() lines 156-198: status 1 on the cancel/refund success branches (including line 92 where cancelOrder returns 1 even though drawbackMoney's boolean result at line 64 was only logged, not used to gate the response), status 0 on order-not-found / cancel-not-permitted branches.
reread: CancelServiceImpl.java private calculateRefund(Order) lines 228-231: Double.parseDouble(order.getPrice()) then arithmetic then DecimalFormat("0.00").

reference records this cech atom narrowly as the enum OrderStatus.getCode() accessor convention; the two available cech-axis candidates describing the order-status-code section instead capture the fuller NOTPAID/PAID/CHANGE branching semantics and are credited to atom:contract:cancel-cancelserviceimpl:requires-orderstatuseligibleforcancel, leaving no distinct candidate for this narrower accessor-convention observation.

reference records that order.setStatus(OrderStatus.CANCEL.getCode()) is set (CancelServiceImpl.java cancelFromOrder() line 240, cancelFromOtherOrder() line 265) before the PUT persists it through ts-order-service or ts-order-other-service; neither pass wrote a state-axis atom for this transition, though the PUT calls themselves are captured as effect atoms elsewhere.
